title: ERC6551 token() and owner() reading immutable args from clone bytecode with cached chain ID fork safety
explanation: ERC6551 clones have (chainId, tokenContract, tokenId) appended at runtime offset 0x4d. (1) extcodecopy reads all 96 bytes in one operation — far cheaper than 3 separate SLOADs (~600 gas vs ~6300 for cold loads). (2) The mload at 0x40 collides with the free memory pointer slot, so cached `m` is restored at the end. (3) For owner(), block.chainid is compared against an immutable cached at construction time — on the wrong fork the cachedChainId mismatches and result stays 0. (4) The ownerOf staticcall is built directly in scratch space: selector at 0x20 (bytes [28..32] = [0x3c..0x40]), tokenId already at 0x40 from extcodecopy. staticcall reads from 0x3c for 0x24 bytes (4-byte selector + 32-byte tokenId). (5) `mul(mload(0x20), and(gt(returndatasize, 0x1f), staticcall(...)))` is the standard return-validating idiom — Yul right-to-left evaluation runs staticcall first, then checks returndata size, AND with success, multiplied with the actual returned address.
when_to_apply: ERC6551 token-bound accounts deployed via the canonical registry where (chainId, tokenContract, tokenId) are appended to the clone bytecode
when_not_to_apply: Custom ERC6551 deployments storing token info in storage, or non-clone implementations
